links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The Japanese waxwing (Bombycilla japonica) is a fairly small passerine bird in the waxwing family found in the eastern Palaearctic, where it breeds south of the breeding range of the related Bohemian waxwing, but overlaps extensively with it in winter

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: thumb|left|Two Japanese waxwings feeding on berries The Japanese waxwing breeds in the dense coniferous forest in the Russian Far East (the Amur basin, northern Primorsky Krai, and Sakhalin) and northeasternmost China (Manchuria, Heilongjiang province)

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: Outside of the breeding season, it migrates south to winter in Japan, the Korean Peninsula, eastern China and Taiwan; the exact distribution is irregular, as birds will travel and move in search of food, mainly seasonal berries, and birds may be common in a certain area during one year but move away the next
